{"id":"medsimples-68fj.4","title":"CogniTest backend: proxy invite endpoints","description":"## What
Add invite-related proxy endpoints to CogniTest backend that forward to MedSimples.

## Context
- CogniTest backend proxies auth/registration calls to MedSimples via medsimples_client.py
- Shared httpx.AsyncClient on app.state.http_client — reuse, never create per-call clients
- Two distinct auth modes for the three endpoints (do NOT conflate them):

| Endpoint | Auth Mode | CogniTest role |
|---|---|---|
| POST /api/v1/invites | Forward user JWT (Bearer) | Proxy with Bearer token |
| GET /api/v1/invites/validate?token=X | No JWT (invitee) | Proxy with X-Webhook-Secret |
| POST /api/v1/invites/accept | No JWT (invitee) | Proxy with X-Webhook-Secret |

- X-Webhook-Secret is a BACKEND-ONLY credential — must NEVER appear in frontend env vars or client-side code
- Error sanitization: 5xx → 502 + Portuguese message, 4xx → forward status + sanitized message
- Cache-Control: no-store on GET /validate and POST /accept responses ONLY (NOT on POST /invites — it's a state-mutating POST, caching not relevant)

## SECURITY FIX REQUIRED: verify_webhook_secret() timing vulnerability
The existing verify_webhook_secret() dependency in cognitest/backend uses direct string comparison (\!=) which is timing-vulnerable. Fix before adding new endpoints that reuse it:
```python
# BEFORE (timing-vulnerable):
if x_webhook_secret \!= settings.cognitest_webhook_secret:
    raise HTTPException(...)

# AFTER (constant-time):
import hmac
if not hmac.compare_digest(x_webhook_secret, settings.cognitest_webhook_secret):
    raise HTTPException(...)
```
This is a one-line fix that improves all existing webhook endpoints simultaneously.

## Testing
- [ ] Fix verify_webhook_secret() to use hmac.compare_digest() FIRST
- [ ] POST /invites with valid admin JWT proxies correctly to MedSimples
- [ ] POST /invites with non-admin JWT returns 403 (forwarded from MedSimples)
- [ ] GET /invites/validate passes token param through, returns masked email metadata
- [ ] POST /invites/accept returns JWT on success
- [ ] MedSimples 409 (duplicate invite or cross-org conflict) forwarded correctly
- [ ] MedSimples 5xx → 502 with generic Portuguese error
- [ ] Cache-Control: no-store present on validate and accept responses
- [ ] Cache-Control: no-store NOT added to POST /invites response
- [ ] X-Webhook-Secret value not accessible from any frontend env var

## Validation
- [ ] No invite logic in CogniTest backend — pure proxy
- [ ] Separate MedSimplesClient methods for JWT-forward vs. webhook-secret calls
- [ ] verify_webhook_secret() uses hmac.compare_digest()
- [ ] X-Webhook-Secret is backend-only (not in PUBLIC_* env vars)

## Files
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/invites.py (new)
- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py (add invite proxy methods)
- cognitest/backend/app/main.py (register router)
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/webhooks.py (fix hmac.compare_digest)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:20Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:30:06Z"}

{"id":"medsimples-68fj.5","title":"CogniTest frontend: invite form","description":"## What
Add an invite form to the CogniTest org settings area so org admins can invite doctors.

## Context
- CogniTest frontend: SvelteKit + TypeScript + Tailwind
- API pattern: fetch(PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL + '/invites', { method: 'POST', headers: {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}, body: JSON.stringify({...}) })
- Error handling: check response.ok, parse data.detail (string or array for FastAPI validation errors)
- JWT in localStorage as 'cognitest_token' — check in onMount for auth guard
- Form styling: use .input, .label, .btn-primary, .card utility classes (defined in app.css)
- i18n: import { _ } from '$lib/i18n', use $_('key.path')

## UI
- Location: new route /settings/team
- Form fields: Email (type=email, trim+lowercase), Name, Role (select: member/manager only — not admin)
- Submit: POST /api/v1/invites
- Success: clear form, show 'Convite enviado para [masked_email]'
- Error 409: 'Já existe um convite pendente para este email' or 'Este email já pertence a outra organização'
- Loading state on button (disabled + spinner) to prevent double-submit

## Access Control
- Auth guard in onMount: if no cognitest_token → goto('/register')
- Role check: read org_role from auth store if available — only fetch /api/v1/auth/me if org_role is not already in the stored session. Avoid the extra network round-trip if the role is in the store.
- Redirect non-admins/managers to dashboard (admins and managers can invite, not just admins)

## i18n Keys (both pt-BR.json and en.json)
- settings.team.title, settings.team.invite_button
- settings.team.email, settings.team.name, settings.team.role
- settings.team.invite_success, settings.team.invite_error_duplicate
- settings.team.invite_error_cross_org (new: 'Este email já pertence a outra organização')
- settings.team.invite_error_generic

## Testing
- [ ] Form renders for org admin user
- [ ] Form renders for org manager user (managers can also invite)
- [ ] Unauthenticated user redirected to /register
- [ ] Non-admin/non-manager redirected to dashboard
- [ ] Successful invite shows success message, clears form
- [ ] 409 shows appropriate error (duplicate vs cross-org)
- [ ] Button disabled during submit (prevents double-submit)
- [ ] Email normalized to lowercase before submit

## Validation
- [ ] Proper Portuguese accents in all i18n strings
- [ ] Client-side email format validation before submit
- [ ] Add navigation link to /settings/team in layout (admin/manager only)
- [ ] Role check reads from store when possible (no unnecessary network call)

## Files
- cognitest/frontend/src/routes/settings/team/+page.svelte (new)
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/i18n/pt-BR.json
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/i18n/en.json
- cognitest/frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte (add nav link)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:45Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:30:27Z"}

{"id":"medsimples-68fj.6","title":"CogniTest frontend: accept invite registration page","description":"## What
Extend the registration page to handle invite-based registration — pre-filled fields, no org creation step, auto-login on success.

## Context
- Existing page: cognitest/frontend/src/routes/register/+page.svelte
- Token in URL: /register?token=X — read via $page.url.searchParams.get('token') (reactive: $: inviteToken = $page.url.searchParams.get('token'))
- Two-phase: GET /api/v1/invites/validate?token=X on page load (safe for email scanners), POST /api/v1/invites/accept on form submit
- On validate response: pre-fill name, show org_name, hide org creation fields (CNPJ, clinic name)
  - NOTE: email field shows the MASKED email from validate response (not editable, not full email)
  - The actual email for submission comes from the user entering it themselves OR from the form value — server validates match
- On accept success: store JWT in localStorage ('cognitest_token', 'cognitest_user_id', 'cognitest_org_id') → goto('/dashboard')
- Token NEVER stored in localStorage — only used in form submit body

## Two-Phase Token Security Notes
- GET /validate returns masked email (e.g. 'j***@example.com') to protect against URL logging/proxies
- The invitee must enter their email on the form; server validates it matches the invite
- This prevents email scanners (Outlook SafeLinks, etc.) from consuming the single-use token on GET
- The actual POST /accept consumes the token atomically

## Edge Cases
- Expired/consumed/invalid token on validate: show error state, hide form entirely. 'Este convite expirou. Entre em contato com o administrador.'
- Existing user being added to org (no org previously): accept succeeds, auto-login as normal
- Existing user already in another org: server returns 409 — show 'Este convite não pode ser aceito. Entre em contato com o administrador.'
- Network error on validate: show generic error, offer retry

## Fields for Invite Registration (beyond name and org)
- Email (must enter, compared server-side to invite.email)
- Password (enforce min 10 chars client-side)
- CRM number
- Phone

## Implementation Notes
- Use onMount + let canceled = false pattern for the async validate call (prevents state update after navigation)
- Show masked email from validate response as hint ('Convite enviado para j***@example.com')
- The email field the user fills in is their own entry — server does the comparison
- Form sends token in request body, never in URL params (token is NOT re-sent in URL on submit)
- If the register page branching (invite mode vs normal mode) becomes complex, consider dedicated route /accept-invite/[token]

## Testing
- [ ] Page with ?token= calls validate on load
- [ ] Name pre-filled from validate response
- [ ] Org name shown, org creation fields (CNPJ, clinic name) hidden
- [ ] Expired token shows error, no form shown
- [ ] Consumed token (double-click) shows error, no form shown
- [ ] 409 on accept (cross-org conflict) shows appropriate error message
- [ ] Successful submit auto-logs in, redirects to dashboard
- [ ] Token not stored in localStorage
- [ ] Cancel token prevents state update if user navigates away during validate

## Validation
- [ ] Form submit sends token in body (not URL)
- [ ] Email field is separate user-entered field (server validates match)
- [ ] Cache-Control: no-store behavior respected (backend sets header; frontend does not cache)

## Files
- cognitest/frontend/src/routes/register/+page.svelte (modify)
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/i18n/pt-BR.json
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/i18n/en.json","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:46Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:30:49Z"}

{"id":"medsimples-68fj.8","title":"fix: accept_invite proxy must set httpOnly cookie after invite acceptance","description":"## Issue
cognitest/backend/app/routers/invites.py accept_invite() returns raw JSON including access_token from MedSimples but does NOT set an httpOnly cookie. The frontend removed localStorage storage (bead 68fj.7), so after acceptance the JWT is discarded and no auth session is established. User is redirected to /dashboard as unauthenticated.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — invite acceptance produces no usable session; the entire flow is broken.

## Location
cognitest/backend/app/routers/invites.py:150-183
Compare with cognitest/backend/app/routers/auth.py:47-54 (register endpoint — correct pattern)

## Why This Matters
Users completing invite registration land on /dashboard with no session. Every subsequent authenticated request returns 401. Feature is non-functional.

## Fix
Mirror the register() pattern in accept_invite():
1. Move _set_auth_cookie() and CT_COOKIE_NAME to a shared module (cognitest/backend/app/auth/cookie.py) to avoid circular imports
2. In accept_invite(): add Response parameter, call _set_auth_cookie(response, token), strip access_token from JSON before returning

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Accept invite returns Set-Cookie: ct_token=...; HttpOnly
- [ ] access_token NOT present in response body
- [ ] /dashboard loads authenticated after invite acceptance
- [ ] Existing register flow still works (regression check)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T19:09:52Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-01T19:09:52Z"}

{"id":"medsimples-68fj.9","title":"fix: token consumed before email validation in accept_invite — burns invite on wrong email","description":"## Issue
In medsimples/backend/app/services/invite_service.py accept_invite(), the token is atomically consumed (status=consumed, consumed_at=now()) BEFORE the submitted email is validated against invite.email. If the invitee submits the wrong email, they get a 422 error but the token is permanently burned. The org admin must issue a new invite.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — UX-breaking bug; also a denial-of-service vector (attacker with the invite URL can burn the token by submitting any email address).

## Location
invite_service.py:232-255 — UPDATE...RETURNING at line 232, email validation at line 251

## Fix
Include the email as a WHERE clause condition in the atomic UPDATE so it only consumes if the email matches. Eliminates the separate email check entirely:

```python
result = self.db.execute(
    update(Invite)
    .where(
        Invite.token_hash == token_hash,
        Invite.status == InviteStatus.pending,
        Invite.expires_at > func.now(),
        Invite.email == normalized_email,   # add this condition
    )
    .values(status=InviteStatus.consumed, consumed_at=func.now(), accepted_from_ip=request_ip)
    .returning(Invite)
)
invite = result.scalars().first()
if invite is None:
    raise ValidationError('Convite inválido, expirado ou e-mail incorreto.')
```
Remove the subsequent hmac.compare_digest() check (now redundant).

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Submitting wrong email returns 404/422 but token remains pending (can retry)
- [ ] Submitting correct email consumes token atomically
- [ ] Two concurrent accepts of the same token: only one succeeds","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T19:09:52Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-01T19:09:52Z"}

{"id":"medsimples-68fj.10","title":"fix: RLS on invites table blocks unauthenticated token-hash lookup in accept flow","description":"## Issue
The invites table has FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY with a policy requiring app.org_id to be set. The accept_invite() endpoint is unauthenticated — it receives a raw token, hashes it, and queries the DB for the invite row. But set_org_rls_context() is called AFTER this lookup (it cannot be called before because we need the invite row to get org_id). With FORCE RLS active and app.org_id unset, current_setting('app.org_id', true) returns NULL, making the WHERE clause org_id = NULL — always false. The invite row is invisible and accept always returns 404.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — accept_invite() can never find the invite row; the flow always fails with 404.

## Location
medsimples/backend/alembic/versions/m1b2c3d4e5f6_add_invites_table.py — RLS policy
medsimples/backend/app/services/invite_service.py:222-246 — token lookup before RLS context

## Fix
Split the single FOR ALL policy into separate policies:
- SELECT: allow lookup by token_hash without org context (for the accept flow):
  ```sql
  CREATE POLICY invites_token_lookup ON invites FOR SELECT
  USING (
    org_id = current_setting('app.org_id', true)::integer
    OR token_hash IS NOT NULL  -- allow unauthenticated token lookups
  );
  ```
  OR more precisely — use a bypass flag:
  ```sql
  USING (
    current_setting('app.is_superadmin', true) = 'true'
    OR org_id = NULLIF(current_setting('app.org_id', true), '')::integer
  )
  ```
  (The NULLIF handles the case where app.org_id is not set — avoids invalid cast error.)

Alternatively, use set_superadmin_context() only for the token lookup SELECT, then re-set org context before writes. This requires careful implementation since set_superadmin_context is forbidden in accept_invite per bead spec.

The safest fix: add a dedicated no-auth RLS policy for the invites SELECT specifically for token lookups.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] GET /invites/validate returns invite metadata (currently fails silently with 404)
- [ ] POST /invites/accept finds invite row before set_org_rls_context is called
- [ ] Authenticated org members still cannot see other orgs' invites","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T19:10:10Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-01T19:10:10Z"}

{"id":"medsimples-68fj.11","title":"fix: org_role column type mismatch — migration creates VARCHAR(20) but model declares Enum(OrgRole)","description":"## Issue
The migration creates org_role as sa.String(20) / VARCHAR(20). The Invite model declares it as Column(Enum(OrgRole)). SQLAlchemy's Enum() with a Python str enum defaults to native_enum=True, which expects a PostgreSQL enum type named 'orgrole'. No such type exists. At runtime, SQLAlchemy may fall back to VARCHAR behavior, but alembic autogenerate will detect a pending change on every run, producing spurious migrations.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — won't break immediately but causes noisy autogenerate diffs and could cause issues if alembic attempts to create the native type.

## Location
medsimples/backend/app/models.py — Invite.org_role column definition
medsimples/backend/alembic/versions/m1b2c3d4e5f6_add_invites_table.py — org_role column

## Fix
Change the model to match the migration explicitly:
```python
org_role = Column(Enum(OrgRole, native_enum=False), nullable=False, default=OrgRole.member)
```
Or alternatively, change the migration to create the orgrole PG type (less preferred, adds a type to manage).

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] alembic revision --autogenerate produces no changes for the invites table
- [ ] invite.org_role can be set and read as OrgRole enum values","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T19:10:30Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-01T19:10:30Z"}

{"id":"medsimples-68fj.13","title":"chore: document invite TTL as intentionally 7 days (not 24h per spec)","description":"## Issue
invite_service.py line 22: _INVITE_TTL_DAYS = 7 but the bead spec explicitly says 'Token expires after 24h (standard MagicToken TTL)'. Either the spec should be updated to document the 7-day decision, or the constant should be changed to 1 day.

## Severity
P3 — documentation discrepancy, no functional bug.

## Fix Options
Option A (preferred): Add inline comment explaining the decision:
  _INVITE_TTL_DAYS = 7  # Longer than magic-link TTL: invitees need time to register
  Update the bead description to reflect the actual TTL.

Option B: Change to _INVITE_TTL_HOURS = 24 to match spec.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Code constant and documentation agree on the TTL value","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T19:10:49Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-01T19:10:49Z"}

{"id":"medsimples-68fj.14","title":"chore: use dedicated invite_hmac_key separate from JWT secret_key","description":"## Issue
invite_service.py uses settings.secret_key as the HMAC key for invite token hashing. This is the same key used for JWT signing. If the key is rotated (security incident), ALL pending invites become cryptographically invalid — their stored token_hash values no longer match.

## Severity  
P3 — operational risk, not an immediate security hole.

## Fix
Add a dedicated setting:
```python
# medsimples/backend/app/settings.py
invite_hmac_key: str = Field(default='', description='Separate HMAC key for invite tokens. Defaults to secret_key if not set.')

@property
def effective_invite_hmac_key(self) -> str:
    return self.invite_hmac_key or self.secret_key
```
Update _hmac_token() in invite_service.py to use settings.effective_invite_hmac_key.
Add to .env.example with a comment.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] invite_hmac_key in .env.example with documentation
- [ ] _hmac_token() uses dedicated key when configured","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T19:10:50Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-01T19:10:50Z"}

{"description":"## What

Add a password login endpoint to the CogniTest backend that proxies to MedSimples and sets an httpOnly auth cookie.

## Context

- MedSimples POST /token uses OAuth2PasswordRequestForm (form-encoded), returns {access_token, token_type}
- Established pattern: call set_auth_cookie(response, token), strip token from response body (see register() in auth.py)
- **CRITICAL: MedSimplesClient.login() must follow the request_magic_link()/create_invite() shared-client pattern — NOT register(), which creates a new AsyncClient per call (known resource leak)**
- **CRITICAL: login() must use httpx data= parameter for form encoding and must NOT call _get_headers() which forces Content-Type: application/json**
- Rate limit: 5/minute per IP (two-layer defense with MedSimples's own rate limiting)
- Proxy error handling: HTTPException for 502, generic 422 for any 4xx; sanitize all upstream errors — never forward verbatim
- On MedSimples 4xx: return 422 {"detail": "E-mail ou senha inválidos."}
- On MedSimples connection failure: return 502 {"detail": "Serviço temporariamente indisponível"}
- LoginRequest needs max_length on password (max_length=128) — prevents 10MB payload forwarding to MedSimples
- Also fix JWT error detail leak in dependencies.py line 47: replace detail=f"Invalid token: {str(e)}" with detail="Authentication required"

## Testing

- [ ] POST /auth/login with valid credentials returns 200, sets ct_token cookie, body has no access_token
- [ ] POST /auth/login with wrong password returns 422 with sanitized message (not upstream error)
- [ ] POST /auth/login with missing fields returns 422
- [ ] POST /auth/login exceeding rate limit returns 429
- [ ] POST /auth/login when MedSimples is unreachable returns 502
- [ ] login() uses shared client (verify no 'async with httpx.AsyncClient()' in login method body)

## Validation

- [ ] ct_token cookie is httpOnly, Secure (in prod), SameSite matches existing set_auth_cookie() behavior
- [ ] access_token never appears in response body
- [ ] Upstream error messages never forwarded verbatim
- [ ] Endpoint registered in main.py router
- [ ] LoginRequest.password has max_length=128
- [ ] dependencies.py line 47 no longer leaks JWT exception detail

## Files

- cognitest/backend/app/routers/auth.py
- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py (login() method — follow request_magic_link pattern)
- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/auth.py (LoginRequest with max_length)
- cognitest/backend/app/auth/dependencies.py (fix JWT error leak on line 47)

## Dependencies

None — standalone backend change
"}

{"description":"## What

Add a dedicated /login page to CogniTest frontend with email+password form, and fix the post-registration/post-login redirect so it lands on the actual app (not the marketing landing page).

## Context

- Current state: goto('/') after register/invite-accept lands on the marketing homepage (routes/+page.svelte), not any app UI — this is a pre-existing bug being fixed here
- There is no dedicated dashboard — the only authenticated app routes are /(app)/commissions and /settings/team
- The login form should live at /login (new route) so it's bookmarkable and shareable
- Auth guard pattern: call GET /auth/me with credentials: 'include'; redirect to /login on 401
- On successful login: the ct_token cookie is set by the backend; the frontend should redirect to /commissions
- Show 'Prefira receber um link? Use o link mágico' hint linking to / for users who have no password
- **Keep the login page simple: ~100 lines, 2 fields (email + password), one error string. Do NOT add password strength meter, fieldErrors dict, multi-step form, or per-field validation — only 2 fields, validate non-empty before submit**
- Error cases: 3 cases only — rate-limited (429), invalid credentials (any 4xx), network error. Do not distinguish between 401/403/422.

## Testing

- [ ] /login page renders email + password fields with visible labels in Portuguese
- [ ] Submit with valid credentials: redirects to /commissions
- [ ] Submit with invalid credentials: shows 'E-mail ou senha inválidos.' inline, clears password field, preserves email
- [ ] Submit with empty fields: client-side validation prevents submit, shows inline error
- [ ] 429 response: shows 'Muitas tentativas. Tente novamente em alguns instantes.'
- [ ] 502 response: shows 'Serviço temporariamente indisponível'
- [ ] Submit button disabled while request in-flight (no double-submit)
- [ ] goto('/commissions') after register (both handleInviteSubmit and handleSubmit in register/+page.svelte)

## Validation

- [ ] /login route exists and is accessible without authentication
- [ ] Post-login redirect lands on /commissions
- [ ] Post-registration redirect (both handlers in register/+page.svelte) lands on /commissions
- [ ] Password field has autocomplete="current-password"
- [ ] credentials: 'include' on the fetch call
- [ ] No access_token stored in localStorage
- [ ] Login page is ~100 lines or less

## Files

- cognitest/frontend/src/routes/login/+page.svelte (new — keep simple)
- cognitest/frontend/src/routes/register/+page.svelte (fix goto target: both handleInviteSubmit and handleSubmit)

## Dependencies

- medsimples-ib8d.1 (backend endpoint must exist first)
"}

- Error cases: 3 cases only — rate-limited (429), invalid credentials (any 4xx), network error. Do not distinguish between 401/403/422.

## Testing

- [ ] /login page renders email + password fields with visible labels in Portuguese
- [ ] Submit with valid credentials: redirects to /commissions
- [ ] Submit with invalid credentials: shows 'E-mail ou senha inválidos.' inline, clears password field, preserves email
- [ ] Submit with empty fields: client-side validation prevents submit, shows inline error
- [ ] 429 response: shows 'Muitas tentativas. Tente novamente em alguns instantes.'
- [ ] 502 response: shows 'Serviço temporariamente indisponível'
- [ ] Submit button disabled while request in-flight (no double-submit)
- [ ] goto('/commissions') after register (both handleInviteSubmit and handleSubmit in register/+page.svelte)

## Validation

- [ ] /login route exists and is accessible without authentication
- [ ] Post-login redirect lands on /commissions
- [ ] Post-registration redirect (both handlers in register/+page.svelte) lands on /commissions
- [ ] Password field has autocomplete="current-password"
- [ ] credentials: 'include' on the fetch call
- [ ] No access_token stored in localStorage
- [ ] Login page is ~100 lines or less

## Files

- cognitest/frontend/src/routes/login/+page.svelte (new — keep simple)
- cognitest/frontend/src/routes/register/+page.svelte (fix goto target: both handleInviteSubmit and handleSubmit)

- medsimples-ib8d.1 (backend endpoint must exist first)

{"id":"medsimples-ib8d.4","title":"Fix silent success when MedSimples returns 200 with no access_token","description":"## Issue
POST /auth/login returns 200 and logs 'cognitest_login_success' even when MedSimples returns a 200 with no access_token in the body. No cookie is set. The frontend sees success, calls goto('/commissions'), the layout guard calls /auth/me, gets 401, and loops back to /login with no error surfaced.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Silent success on missing token makes login appear to work while not actually logging the user in.

## Location
cognitest/backend/app/routers/auth.py lines 99-105

## Fix
```python
token = body.get('access_token')
if not token:
    logger.error('login_proxy_missing_token', upstream_status=status_code)
    raise HTTPException(status_code=502, detail='Serviço temporariamente indisponível', headers=_NO_STORE)
set_auth_cookie(response, token)
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] POST /auth/login with mocked MedSimples 200 response missing access_token returns 502
- [ ] login_proxy_missing_token is logged
- [ ] No cognitest_login_success log on token-less response","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T01:27:27Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-02T01:27:27Z","labels":["review","security"]}

{"id":"medsimples-ib8d.5","title":"Fix rate limiter key_func: uses get_remote_address instead of get_real_ip","description":"## Issue
The router-level Limiter in auth.py uses get_remote_address which reads request.client.host (always the load balancer IP behind a proxy). The app-level limiter correctly uses a custom get_real_ip that walks X-Forwarded-For. Result: all users behind the same proxy share one rate limit bucket, and an attacker with a spoofed X-Forwarded-For bypasses per-IP limiting entirely.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Rate limiting is effectively non-functional in production (reverse proxy deployment).

## Location
cognitest/backend/app/routers/auth.py — the local Limiter instance:
```python
limiter = Limiter(key_func=get_remote_address)  # WRONG in proxy deployments
```

## Fix
Replace with the same get_real_ip function used by app.state.limiter in main.py, or use the app-level limiter instead of a separate router-level instance.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Rate limit applies per real client IP, not per proxy IP
- [ ] X-Forwarded-For spoofing from untrusted proxies is rejected
- [ ] get_real_ip from main.py (or equivalent) used in the login rate limiter","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T01:27:28Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-02T01:27:28Z","labels":["review","security"]}

{"id":"medsimples-ib8d.8","title":"Add missing guard at top of handleSubmit against re-entrant calls","description":"## Issue
handleSubmit in login/+page.svelte has no re-entrancy guard at its entry point. The form's on:submit and the Enter key handler (handleKeyDown) can both call handleSubmit concurrently in a narrow timing window (e.g. user focuses submit button and hits Space + Enter simultaneously). The loading flag check in handleKeyDown is correct but handleSubmit itself has no guard.

## Severity
P2 — Can cause duplicate login requests in edge cases.

## Location
cognitest/frontend/src/routes/login/+page.svelte — handleSubmit()

## Fix
```typescript
async function handleSubmit(): Promise<void> {
    if (loading) return;  // add this as first line
    // ...
}
```

Also: the handleKeyDown function is redundant — the form's on:submit already handles Enter natively. Remove handleKeyDown entirely and the on:keydown handler from the form.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] if (loading) return; added as first line of handleSubmit
- [ ] handleKeyDown function removed (or kept with .catch() attached)
- [ ] Form submits correctly via Enter key (native form behavior)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T01:27:53Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-02T01:27:53Z","labels":["frontend","review"]}

{"id":"medsimples-ib8d.9","title":"Simplify login endpoint: consolidate repeated patterns","description":"## Issue
The login endpoint in routers/auth.py has several redundant patterns:
1. Cache-Control header set twice (via _NO_STORE on each HTTPException AND manually on success path) — set once at top of function instead
2. except Exception on ms_response.json() should be except ValueError (json.JSONDecodeError is a subclass)
3. Bare 'as e' binding in auth/dependencies.py catch block is unused after the JWT leak fix — remove it
4. body.pop('access_token', None); return body is simpler than the dict comprehension

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE — Code clarity and consistency.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/auth.py (login endpoint)
- cognitest/backend/app/auth/dependencies.py (unused 'as e')

## Fix
```python
# auth.py: set Cache-Control once at top
response.headers['Cache-Control'] = 'no-store'
# Remove headers=_NO_STORE from each HTTPException raise

# auth.py: narrow exception type
except ValueError:  # not except Exception

# auth.py: simpler token strip
body.pop('access_token', None)
return body

# dependencies.py: remove unused binding
except (JWTError, ValueError):  # not 'as e'
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] No behavior change
- [ ] Cache-Control set once at function start
- [ ] except ValueError instead of except Exception on json parse
- [ ] Dict comprehension replaced with body.pop()","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T01:28:17Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-02T01:28:17Z","labels":["review","simplicity"]}

If the default authenticated route ever changes, it requires a find-and-replace across files. export const DEFAULT_POST_LOGIN_ROUTE = '/commissions';

3. (Optional) Add ?next= support in /(app)/+layout.ts redirect and consume in login page

- [ ] All 3 goto calls reference the constant","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T01:28:18Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-02T01:28:18Z","labels":["frontend","review"]}

{"id":"medsimples-ib8d.12","title":"Login succeeds but /auth/me returns 401 immediately after — cookie not sent on first post-login request","description":"## Issue
Login returns 200 and sets the ct_token cookie, but the immediately following GET /auth/me (from the /(app)/+layout.ts auth guard) returns 401. The cookie is not being sent on that first request after navigation.

## Observed in production logs
```
cognitest_login_success email=paypal@dinaventures.com
POST /api/v1/auth/login 200 OK
GET /api/v1/auth/me 401 Unauthorized
```

## Root cause hypothesis
SameSite=Strict was changed to SameSite=Lax (commit 3ffb183) as an attempted fix, and the auth guard redirect was moved outside the try/catch (commit 8ce819a). These may not have fully resolved the issue — the cookie may still not be sent on the first request after the POST->navigation sequence.

## What to investigate
- Confirm whether the Set-Cookie response header is present on the /auth/login 200 response
- Confirm whether the cookie is present in the browser after login (DevTools -> Application -> Cookies)
- Check if the /auth/me request includes the Cookie header (DevTools -> Network)
- Check cookie domain/path — in production the app is served from a single origin; confirm ct_token path='/' and no domain restriction that would block it
- Check if goto('/commissions') triggers a full page reload or client-side navigation — a full reload would clear in-flight cookies on some browsers

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] After successful login, /auth/me returns 200 (not 401)
- [ ] User lands on /commissions without being redirected back to /login
- [ ] ct_token cookie visible in DevTools after login","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T02:06:11Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-02T02:06:11Z"}

{"description":"## What

Add GET /api/v1/patients/search?q= endpoint to CogniTest backend. Proxies to MedSimples GET /patients?search={q} by forwarding the doctor's Bearer token (not the webhook secret — MedSimples /patients is RLS-gated by the user's own token).

## Context

- MedSimples GET /patients?search= is protected by get_current_user_with_rls (RLS, not webhook secret)
- CogniTest frontend never calls MedSimples directly — must go through CogniTest backend
- The ct_token cookie IS a valid MedSimples JWT — forward it as Bearer in the Authorization header
- MedSimples patient fields: id (int), name (str), birth (date), sex, email1, tel1
- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py already has _get_headers() for service-to-service; add a new bearer_headers(token) method instead
- Add new method to MedSimplesClient: search_patients(q: str, bearer_token: str) -> list[dict]
- Endpoint requires authentication (Depends(get_current_doctor))
- Rate limit: use doctor.user_id as key (not IP) — authenticated endpoint, per-IP breaks behind NAT

### Research Insights

**httpx async pattern:**
- Use shared httpx.AsyncClient from request.app.state.http_client (configured in lifespan), NOT a per-request client
- Per-request clients incur TLS handshake cost (~50-150ms) every call
- Configure in main.py lifespan: app.state.http_client = httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0, limits=Limits(max_connections=100))

**Graceful degradation — IMPORTANT:**
- Return empty list for 404 (no results) and for network errors (MedSimples unreachable)
- But also set a response header X-Upstream-Available: false when MedSimples is unreachable so the frontend can show a degraded-mode warning rather than silently showing no results

**Security — validate response schema:**
- Validate each item from MedSimples against PatientSearchResult Pydantic schema before returning
- Prevents unexpected MedSimples fields from leaking to frontend
- model_validate each item, skip items that fail validation

**Query parameter validation:**
- Query(min_length=2, max_length=200) — names are never longer than 200 chars
- Add regex to reject non-printable characters (httpx URL-encodes them but validate explicitly)

**Rate limiting key:**
- Use doctor.user_id, not IP — authenticated endpoint
- 60/minute is more appropriate if typeahead search is planned (20/min exhausted in 2s of typing)
- Keep 20/minute as default, raise to 60/minute if debounce is confirmed at 300ms+

**Architecture note:**
- Long-term better pattern: use X-Webhook-Secret service account (consistent with get_patient, get_doctor)
- For now, token forwarding is acceptable since ct_token IS a MedSimples token and RLS correctly scopes it
- Do NOT log the Authorization header value (token leakage risk)

## Implementation

Add to cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py:
  async def search_patients(self, q: str, bearer_token: str, http_client: httpx.AsyncClient) -> list[dict]
  - GET {self.base_url}/patients?search={q}
  - Authorization: Bearer {bearer_token} header
  - Returns list of PatientSearchResult validated dicts, empty list on any error
  - Sets returned flag for X-Upstream-Available header

New file: cognitest/backend/app/routers/patients.py
  GET /patients/search?q= (min 2 chars, max 200 chars)
  - Requires get_current_doctor dependency
  - Rate limit key: doctor.user_id (not IP)
  - Extracts bearer token from cookie (request.cookies.get(CT_COOKIE_NAME))
  - Calls client.search_patients(q, bearer_token, request.app.state.http_client)
  - Sets X-Upstream-Available: false header if MedSimples unreachable

{"description":"## What

Extend SessionResponse and the list_sessions endpoint to include the patient's cached_name so the dashboard can show patient names without a second round-trip per session.

## Context

- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py list_sessions does db.query(TestSession) with no relationship loading
- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py has PatientInfo schema (id, cached_name, cached_dob) but NOT used in SessionResponse
- cognitest/backend/app/models/test_session.py: relationship to Patient already declared (line 54): patient = relationship('Patient', ...)
- TestSession.patient_id is a UUID FK to Patient.id, nullable=True
- Fix: add selectinload(TestSession.patient) to list_sessions query, add patient: PatientInfo | None to SessionResponse

### Research Insights

**selectinload vs joinedload — use selectinload here:**
- selectinload issues a 2nd SELECT ... WHERE id IN (...) — for limit=5 this means 5 UUIDs in one IN clause (not N+1)
- joinedload uses JOIN and can cause Cartesian explosion when combined with LIMIT (SQLAlchemy adds subquery wrapping as compensation — extra planner overhead)
- selectinload = 2 queries total regardless of limit value. Correct choice.

**Options order is critical:**
- .options() MUST come before .filter() and before .offset()/.limit()
- Count BEFORE applying offset/limit to avoid pagination breakage

**WCAG / UX note:**
- gray-400 status dots fail WCAG AA on white — always pair with text label, never rely on dot color alone

**Frontend: use Promise.all for dashboard calls:**
- The 3 dashboard API calls (commissions, sessions, definitions) are fully independent
- Sequential awaits = sum of RTTs; Promise.all = slowest of the three
- This belongs in the frontend dashboard page but is noted here for the implementor of 0ekz.3

**Performance: add index on TestSession.created_at:**
- list_sessions orders by created_at DESC
- Without an index, Postgres sorts the full table before LIMIT
- Add composite index (status, created_at) to satisfy both filter and sort in one index scan
- Include this migration in this bead or bead 0ekz.5

## Implementation

In cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py:
  Add patient: PatientInfo | None = None to SessionResponse

In cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py list_sessions:
  from sqlalchemy.orm import selectinload
  query = db.query(TestSession).options(selectinload(TestSession.patient))
  (options BEFORE filter, count BEFORE offset/limit)

In cognitest/backend/app/models/test_session.py:
  Verify relationship to Patient exists — it should at line 54

Optional but recommended: add Alembic migration for composite index (status, created_at) on test_sessions.

## Testing

- [ ] GET /api/v1/sessions/ returns items with patient.cached_name populated (when patient exists)
- [ ] Sessions with no patient record return patient: null (not 500)
- [ ] Pagination and filtering still work correctly
- [ ] SQL echo confirms exactly 2 queries (not N+1) for a 5-item page

## Validation

- [ ] SessionResponse.patient.cached_name is a non-null string for sessions with cached patients
- [ ] No N+1 queries (selectinload not lazy load)
- [ ] count() called before offset/limit (pagination correct)

## Files

- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py
- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py
- cognitest/backend/app/models/test_session.py (verify relationship)
- cognitest/backend/alembic/versions/ (optional: add index on test_sessions.created_at)"}

{"description":"## What

Extend POST /api/v1/sessions/ to accept a medsimples_patient_id (integer) in addition to existing patient_id (UUID). When medsimples_patient_id is provided, backend looks up or creates a CogniTest Patient record, then uses that Patient.id for session creation.

## Context

- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py SessionCreate currently requires patient_id: UUID
- cognitest/backend/app/models/patient.py Patient has medsimples_patient_id field and UniqueConstraint(organization_id, medsimples_patient_id)
- Dashboard patient search returns MedSimples integer IDs — frontend has no way to know the CogniTest Patient UUID
- Organization linking: doctor.org_id (str from JWT) is the MedSimples org integer ID

### Critical Security Findings from Review

**CRITICAL: create_session has no authentication — IDOR on patient_id (sessions.py:120)**
- Current create_session has no get_current_doctor dependency
- Any caller who knows a valid CogniTest patient.id UUID can create sessions for any patient
- Fix: add Depends(get_current_doctor) to create_session AND enforce patient.organization_id == doctor.org_id after patient lookup

**CRITICAL: get_or_create_default_org is wrong for multi-tenant (sessions.py:33)**
- db.query(Organization).first() returns whichever org comes first — NOT the authenticated doctor's org
- All sessions created via this fallback go under one arbitrary org regardless of who is authenticated
- Fix: derive organization_id from get_current_doctor() — look up CogniTest Organization by medsimples_org_id == int(doctor.org_id)
- Only use the default org fallback in single-org dev mode, guarded by settings.dev_mode flag

**TOCTOU in get-or-create Patient — must use IntegrityError retry:**
- The UNIQUE(org_id, medsimples_patient_id) constraint prevents data corruption from concurrent requests
- BUT without catching IntegrityError and retrying with SELECT, the second concurrent request returns 500 to the user
- Correct pattern:
  try:
      db.add(Patient(...))
      db.flush()
  except IntegrityError:
      db.rollback()
      patient = db.query(Patient).filter_by(org_id=org_id, medsimples_patient_id=ms_patient_id).one()

- The MedSimples API call to fetch name/dob must happen BEFORE the transaction, not inside it (avoids holding transaction open during HTTP call)

### Research Insights

**Pydantic validator for XOR (patient_id OR medsimples_patient_id):**
Use model_validator(mode='after') in Pydantic v2:
  @model_validator(mode='after')
  def exactly_one_patient_identifier(self) -> 'SessionCreate':
      has_local = self.patient_id is not None
      has_remote = self.medsimples_patient_id is not None
      if has_local == has_remote:
          raise ValueError('Provide exactly one of patient_id or medsimples_patient_id')
      return self

**Async httpx for MedSimplesClient:**
- get_patient() currently creates a new httpx.AsyncClient per call (TLS handshake cost per call)
- Pass request.app.state.http_client through to avoid per-request client creation
- MedSimples API call to fetch name/dob should run BEFORE starting the DB transaction

## Implementation

In cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py SessionCreate:
  Add: medsimples_patient_id: int | None = None
  Add model_validator(mode='after') for XOR constraint

In cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py create_session:
  1. Add Depends(get_current_doctor) — REQUIRED for IDOR fix
  2. If medsimples_patient_id provided:
     a. Resolve org: query Organization by medsimples_org_id == int(doctor.org_id)
        - Raise 422 if org not found (don't fall back to wrong org)
        - Allow dev_mode fallback only if settings.dev_mode is True
     b. Fetch patient data from MedSimples BEFORE transaction (async, don't block transaction)
     c. get-or-create Patient with IntegrityError retry pattern
  3. Enforce patient.organization_id == org.id after resolution
  4. Use patient.id as session patient_id

## Testing

- [ ] POST /sessions/ with medsimples_patient_id=8 creates session and new Patient record
- [ ] Second call with same medsimples_patient_id reuses existing Patient (no duplicate)
- [ ] Concurrent requests for same medsimples_patient_id: one creates, one fetches — both succeed (no 500)
- [ ] POST /sessions/ with patient_id (UUID) still works unchanged
- [ ] POST /sessions/ with neither patient_id nor medsimples_patient_id returns 422
- [ ] POST /sessions/ with both patient_id and medsimples_patient_id returns 422
- [ ] Unauthenticated POST /sessions/ returns 401 (not 422)
- [ ] Doctor from org A cannot create session for patient in org B (IDOR test)
- [ ] If MedSimples fetch fails, session still created with cached_name=None

## Validation

- [ ] Session created successfully with medsimples_patient_id input
- [ ] Patient.cached_name populated from MedSimples fetch
- [ ] No duplicate Patient records for same (org, medsimples_patient_id) even under concurrent load
- [ ] organization_id derived from authenticated doctor, not db.query(Organization).first()

## Files

- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py
- cognitest/backend/app/models/patient.py (verify medsimples_patient_id field)
- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py (use existing get_patient method)

## Dependencies

- Requires medsimples-0ekz.5 (UUID→Integer migration) for Organization.medsimples_org_id lookup to work"}

{"description":"## What

Change Organization.medsimples_org_id from UUID to Integer and Patient.medsimples_patient_id from UUID to Integer. Add Alembic migration. Also add index on test_sessions.created_at.

## Context

- cognitest/backend/app/models/organization.py: medsimples_org_id = Column(UUID(), nullable=True, unique=True, index=True)
- cognitest/backend/app/models/patient.py: medsimples_patient_id = Column(UUID(), nullable=True)
- MedSimples org IDs are integers (e.g. 36). JWT org_id claim is '36' (string of integer).
- Organization.medsimples_org_id lookup in session create (0ekz.4) requires this to be Integer
- Same root cause as medsimples-jsx8 (doctor_id/organization_id UUID mismatch)
- Check alembic history — latest is 07c4a1e01d85. New migration must come after that.
- Commission.organization_id is a FK to organizations.id (UUID PK) — that is fine, don't change it

### Research Insights

**Correct Alembic pattern for UUID→Integer type change:**
- Use op.execute() with raw SQL, NOT op.alter_column() — the latter cannot handle USING clause
- Always wrap in: if connection.dialect.name == 'postgresql': (consistent with existing migrations)
- Order: drop constraints/indexes → alter type → recreate constraints/indexes
- For NULL-only columns: USING NULL::INTEGER (no complex casting needed)
- Add safety check before altering: SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE column IS NOT NULL — raise RuntimeError if non-zero

**Partial unique index for nullable columns:**
Add WHERE column IS NOT NULL to unique constraint so NULLs don't conflict with each other:
  ALTER TABLE organizations ADD CONSTRAINT organizations_medsimples_org_id_key
  UNIQUE (medsimples_org_id) WHERE medsimples_org_id IS NOT NULL

**Downgrade: leave empty (lossy conversion):**
UUID→Integer is irreversible without data loss. Document and leave downgrade() as pass.

**Also add in this migration:**
- Composite index on test_sessions (status, created_at) for session list query performance
- Allows Postgres to satisfy both status filter AND created_at sort in one index scan

## Implementation

1. In cognitest/backend/app/models/organization.py:
   medsimples_org_id = Column(Integer, nullable=True, unique=True, index=True)

2. In cognitest/backend/app/models/patient.py:
   medsimples_patient_id = Column(Integer, nullable=True)

3. New alembic migration (after 07c4a1e01d85):
   - Safety check: verify no non-NULL values exist
   - Drop unique constraint + index on organizations.medsimples_org_id
   - Drop unique constraint + index on patients.medsimples_patient_id (if any)
   - ALTER COLUMN medsimples_org_id TYPE INTEGER USING NULL::INTEGER
   - ALTER COLUMN medsimples_patient_id TYPE INTEGER USING NULL::INTEGER
   - Recreate unique constraints as partial (WHERE IS NOT NULL)
   - Recreate indexes
   - Add: CREATE INDEX ix_test_sessions_status_created_at ON test_sessions (status, created_at)
   - downgrade: pass (document irreversibility)

## Testing

- [ ] alembic upgrade head succeeds
- [ ] Organization can be looked up by medsimples_org_id=36 (integer)
- [ ] Patient can be looked up by medsimples_patient_id=8 (integer)
- [ ] alembic downgrade -1 does not crash (pass is valid)
- [ ] EXPLAIN on sessions list query shows index scan on ix_test_sessions_status_created_at

## Validation

- [ ] Organization.medsimples_org_id is Integer type in DB
- [ ] Patient.medsimples_patient_id is Integer type in DB
- [ ] Unique constraint preserved (partial, WHERE IS NOT NULL)
- [ ] Existing rows unaffected (dev DB is empty for these tables)

## Files

- cognitest/backend/app/models/organization.py
- cognitest/backend/app/models/patient.py
- cognitest/backend/alembic/versions/019_fix_medsimples_id_types_to_integer.py (NEW)"}

{"description":"## What

Build /dashboard route as the post-login landing page for CogniTest doctors. 2x2 card grid (single column on mobile). Replace /commissions as DEFAULT_POST_LOGIN_ROUTE.

Use the frontend-design and design-taste skills for all UI work.

## Context

- Stack: SvelteKit + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + svelte-i18n
- Existing CSS classes: .card, .btn-primary, .btn-secondary, .btn-lg, .input, .label
- All API calls via PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL (relative /api/v1 in prod)
- CreditsWidget.svelte exists — include it
- Auth layout returns {} — dashboard page must fetch /auth/me in onMount for doctor identity

### Design System (from design-taste-frontend skill)

**Page background:** bg-slate-100 (NOT white — creates visible card separation)
**Card surface:** bg-white rounded-xl border border-slate-200
**Shadow:** shadow-[0_1px_4px_0_rgba(15,23,42,0.06)] hover:shadow-[0_4px_12px_0_rgba(15,23,42,0.08)]
**Grid:** grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-5 max-w-4xl mx-auto
**Card padding:** p-6
**Page outer:** px-4 sm:px-6 py-8 min-h-[100dvh] (use 100dvh not h-screen — prevents iOS Safari layout jump)

**Section label:** text-[11px] font-semibold text-slate-400 uppercase tracking-widest
**KPI value:** text-[32px] font-bold text-slate-900 font-mono tabular-nums
**Body text:** text-[13px] text-slate-600
**Card title:** text-sm font-semibold text-slate-700

**Status dot colors (all WCAG AA on white):**
- completed: bg-green-600 (#16a34a, ratio 4.6:1)
- in_progress: bg-primary-600 (#2563eb, ratio 4.7:1)
- awaiting_payment: bg-amber-600 (#d97706, ratio 4.6:1) — NOT amber-500 (fails WCAG)
- abandoned/cancelled: bg-gray-400 (#9ca3af, ratio 2.9:1 FAILS) — pair with text label, never rely on dot alone

**Card load animation (staggered, CSS only):**
  @keyframes slideUp { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(8px); } to { opacity: 1; } }
  .dashboard-card { animation: slideUp 0.28s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) both; }
  nth-child delays: 0ms, 60ms, 120ms, 180ms

**ANTI-EMOJI policy: no emoji anywhere (from design-taste skill)**
**ANTI-EMOJI: replace all emoji with SVG icons**

### Performance

**CRITICAL: Use Promise.all for all dashboard API calls:**
- commissions, sessions, tests/definitions are fully independent
- Sequential awaits = sum of RTTs. Promise.all = slowest of the three (60-70% faster)
- In SvelteKit: use load() in +page.ts or Promise.all in onMount

### Modal patterns (from SvelteKit research)

**Debounce:** setTimeout + clearTimeout, 300ms, min 2 chars before firing
**Focus trap:** keydown listener traversing focusable elements, Escape to close
**Backdrop:** click on backdrop closes; click on modal content does not
**Transition:** fade for backdrop (150ms), fly for modal panel (y: 50, 200ms)
**Clipboard:** navigator.clipboard.writeText() with execCommand fallback

### Cards

#### Card 1: Revenue (top-left) — bg-white
- Fetch GET /api/v1/commissions/current-month

#### Card 2: Sessões Recentes (top-right) — bg-white

- Opens inline modal (not a new route)
- Modal flow: search patient (debounced 300ms, min 2 chars) → select → pick test → confirm → show link + copy button
- Patient search calls GET /api/v1/patients/search?q= (from bead 0ekz.1)
- Session create posts with medsimples_patient_id (from bead 0ekz.4)
- On success: auto-copy link to clipboard + show success notice (NOT a toast — inline in modal)
- active:scale-[0.98] on buttons for tactile feedback

#### Card 4: Testes Disponíveis (bottom-right) — bg-white
- Fetch GET /api/v1/tests/definitions?active_only=true
- Pill tags: bg-primary-50 text-primary-700 border border-primary-100 rounded-md
- If domain categories available: 3px left border in category color per pill
- Footer: count of active tests
- Empty state: 'Nenhum teste disponível'

## i18n

{"description":"## What

Add GET /api/v1/patients/search?q= endpoint to CogniTest backend. Proxies to MedSimples GET /patients?search={q} by forwarding the doctor's Bearer token (ct_token cookie).

## Context

- MedSimples GET /patients?search= is protected by get_current_user_with_rls (RLS, not webhook secret)
- CogniTest frontend never calls MedSimples directly — must go through CogniTest backend
- The ct_token cookie IS a valid MedSimples JWT — forward it as Bearer in the Authorization header
- MedSimples patient fields: id (int), name (str), birth (date), sex, email1, tel1
- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py already has _get_headers() for service-to-service; add a new bearer_headers(token) method instead
- Add new method to MedSimplesClient: search_patients(q: str, bearer_token: str, http_client: httpx.AsyncClient) -> list[dict]
- Endpoint requires authentication (Depends(get_current_doctor))

### Simplicity (UPDATED: dropped over-engineering)

- **NO X-Upstream-Available header** — return empty list [] on any MedSimples error (network, 4xx, 5xx). Simple and sufficient for v1.
- **NO PatientSearchResult Pydantic schema** — passthrough list[dict] from MedSimples directly. Pydantic validation of a trusted upstream adds fragility with no benefit.
- **NO schemas/patient.py file** — not needed without the schema
- **Use IP-based rate limiting** (existing slowapi pattern via get_remote_address) — do NOT implement per-user key function. 300ms frontend debounce already limits abuse surface. Per-user rate limiting requires custom key boilerplate not justified for v1.

### SSRF Prevention (UPDATED)

- Validate settings.medsimples_api_url at startup against expected hostname format (http/https scheme, no redirects)
- Set allow_redirects=False (or follow_redirects=False in httpx) on the shared AsyncClient — prevents redirect-based SSRF
- Do NOT log the Authorization header value (token leakage risk)

### Shared Client (CRITICAL)

- **search_patients MUST use self._shared_client** — do NOT follow the per-request pattern in get_patient() (line 110-113) which creates a new httpx.AsyncClient per call (TLS handshake ~50-150ms each)
- Check self._shared_client is not None and use it; only fall back to per-request client if explicitly unavailable
- Configure in main.py lifespan: app.state.http_client = httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0, follow_redirects=False)

### httpx async pattern:

- Use shared httpx.AsyncClient from request.app.state.http_client (configured in lifespan), NOT a per-request client
- Per-request clients incur TLS handshake cost (~50-150ms) every call

### Graceful degradation:

- Return empty list for any error (404, network errors, MedSimples unreachable) — log the error server-side only
- No response headers for degraded state (v1 simplicity)

### Query parameter validation:

- Query(min_length=2, max_length=200)
- Add regex to reject non-printable characters

## Implementation

Add to cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py:
  async def search_patients(self, q: str, bearer_token: str, http_client: httpx.AsyncClient) -> list[dict]
  - GET {self.base_url}/patients?search={q}
  - Authorization: Bearer {bearer_token} header
  - Returns raw list[dict] from MedSimples, empty list on any error
  - MUST use self._shared_client (not per-request client)

New file: cognitest/backend/app/routers/patients.py
  GET /patients/search?q= (min 2 chars, max 200 chars)
  - Requires get_current_doctor dependency
  - Rate limit: IP-based (existing get_remote_address pattern)
  - Extracts bearer token from cookie (request.cookies.get(CT_COOKIE_NAME))
  - Returns list[dict] passthrough from MedSimples

Register router in cognitest/backend/app/main.py
Also: configure shared httpx.AsyncClient in app lifespan with follow_redirects=False if not already done.

## Testing

- [ ] GET /patients/search?q=Jo returns list of patients matching 'Jo'
- [ ] GET /patients/search?q=x (1 char) returns 422 validation error
- [ ] No bearer token (unauthenticated) returns 401
- [ ] MedSimples unavailable returns empty list (not 500)
- [ ] Rate limiting active on endpoint
- [ ] Authorization header value is never logged

## Validation

- [ ] Endpoint exists at GET /api/v1/patients/search
- [ ] Bearer token forwarded correctly (uses ct_token cookie value)
- [ ] Does NOT use webhook secret for this endpoint
- [ ] Returns empty list (not error) when MedSimples is unreachable
- [ ] search_patients uses self._shared_client (confirmed in code review)
- [ ] follow_redirects=False on shared client

## Files

- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/patients.py (NEW)
- cognitest/backend/app/main.py (lifespan shared client + router registration)"}

{"id":"medsimples-0ekz.1","title":"Backend: GET /api/v1/patients/search — proxy patient search to MedSimples","description":"## What

Add GET /api/v1/patients/search?q= endpoint to CogniTest backend. Proxies to MedSimples GET /patients?search={q} by forwarding the doctor's Bearer token (not the webhook secret — MedSimples /patients is RLS-gated by the user's own token).

## Context

- MedSimples GET /patients?search= is protected by get_current_user_with_rls (RLS, not webhook secret)
- CogniTest frontend never calls MedSimples directly — must go through CogniTest backend
- The ct_token cookie IS a valid MedSimples JWT — forward it as Bearer in the Authorization header
- MedSimples patient fields: id (int), name (str), birth (date), sex, email1, tel1
- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py already has _get_headers() for service-to-service; add a new bearer_headers(token) method instead
- Add new method to MedSimplesClient: search_patients(q: str, bearer_token: str) -> list[dict]
- Endpoint requires authentication (Depends(get_current_doctor))
- Rate limit: use doctor.user_id as key (not IP) — authenticated endpoint, per-IP breaks behind NAT

### Research Insights

**httpx async pattern:**
- Use shared httpx.AsyncClient from request.app.state.http_client (configured in lifespan), NOT a per-request client
- Per-request clients incur TLS handshake cost (~50-150ms) every call
- Configure in main.py lifespan: app.state.http_client = httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0, limits=Limits(max_connections=100))

**Graceful degradation — IMPORTANT:**
- Return empty list for 404 (no results) and for network errors (MedSimples unreachable)
- But also set a response header X-Upstream-Available: false when MedSimples is unreachable so the frontend can show a degraded-mode warning rather than silently showing no results

**Security — validate response schema:**
- Validate each item from MedSimples against PatientSearchResult Pydantic schema before returning
- Prevents unexpected MedSimples fields from leaking to frontend
- model_validate each item, skip items that fail validation

**Query parameter validation:**
- Query(min_length=2, max_length=200) — names are never longer than 200 chars
- Add regex to reject non-printable characters (httpx URL-encodes them but validate explicitly)

**Rate limiting key:**
- Use doctor.user_id, not IP — authenticated endpoint
- 60/minute is more appropriate if typeahead search is planned (20/min exhausted in 2s of typing)
- Keep 20/minute as default, raise to 60/minute if debounce is confirmed at 300ms+

**Architecture note:**
- Long-term better pattern: use X-Webhook-Secret service account (consistent with get_patient, get_doctor)
- For now, token forwarding is acceptable since ct_token IS a MedSimples token and RLS correctly scopes it
- Do NOT log the Authorization header value (token leakage risk)

## Implementation

Add to cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py:
  async def search_patients(self, q: str, bearer_token: str, http_client: httpx.AsyncClient) -> list[dict]
  - GET {self.base_url}/patients?search={q}
  - Authorization: Bearer {bearer_token} header
  - Returns list of PatientSearchResult validated dicts, empty list on any error
  - Sets returned flag for X-Upstream-Available header

New file: cognitest/backend/app/routers/patients.py
  GET /patients/search?q= (min 2 chars, max 200 chars)
  - Requires get_current_doctor dependency
  - Rate limit key: doctor.user_id (not IP)
  - Extracts bearer token from cookie (request.cookies.get(CT_COOKIE_NAME))
  - Calls client.search_patients(q, bearer_token, request.app.state.http_client)
  - Sets X-Upstream-Available: false header if MedSimples unreachable
  - Returns list[PatientSearchResult] schema: {id: int, name: str, birth: str | None}

New schema: cognitest/backend/app/schemas/patient.py
  PatientSearchResult(BaseModel): id: int, name: str, birth: str | None

Register router in cognitest/backend/app/main.py

Also: configure shared httpx.AsyncClient in app lifespan if not already done.

## Testing

- [ ] GET /patients/search?q=Jo returns list of patients matching 'Jo'
- [ ] GET /patients/search?q=x (1 char) returns 422 validation error
- [ ] No bearer token (unauthenticated) returns 401
- [ ] MedSimples unavailable returns empty list + X-Upstream-Available: false header
- [ ] Response items conform to PatientSearchResult schema (no extra MedSimples fields)
- [ ] Rate limiting uses user identity, not IP (two users behind same NAT get separate limits)

## Validation

- [ ] Endpoint exists at GET /api/v1/patients/search
- [ ] Bearer token forwarded correctly (uses ct_token cookie value)
- [ ] Does NOT use webhook secret for this endpoint
- [ ] Returns empty list (not error) when MedSimples is unreachable
- [ ] X-Upstream-Available: false header set on degraded response
- [ ] Authorization header value is never logged

## Files

- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/patients.py (NEW)
- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/patient.py (NEW)
- cognitest/backend/app/main.py (lifespan shared client + router registration)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T03:25:01Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:33:54Z"}

{"description":"## What

Extend SessionResponse and the list_sessions endpoint to include the patient's cached_name so the dashboard can show patient names without a second round-trip per session.

## Context

- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py list_sessions does db.query(TestSession) with no relationship loading
- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py has PatientInfo schema (id, cached_name, cached_dob) but NOT used in SessionResponse
- cognitest/backend/app/models/test_session.py: relationship to Patient already declared (line 54): patient = relationship('Patient', ...)
- TestSession.patient_id is a UUID FK to Patient.id, nullable=True
- Fix: add selectinload(TestSession.patient) to list_sessions query, add patient: PatientInfo | None to SessionResponse

### CRITICAL: Auth + Org Scoping Required (UPDATED)

- list_sessions currently has NO Depends(get_current_doctor) — returns sessions across ALL organizations
- This must be fixed in this bead, not deferred: add Depends(get_current_doctor) to list_sessions
- Add WHERE clinician_user_id = doctor.user_id filter to scope results to the authenticated doctor
- Without this: (a) dashboard shows wrong data, (b) total = query.count() is a full table scan on all sessions, (c) session UUIDs are publicly enumerable (security issue)

### Dashboard: Skip count() for the recent sessions card

- The dashboard card shows 'last 5 sessions' only — pagination metadata (total count) is unnecessary
- For limit=5 dashboard queries, skip the count() round-trip: return rows only, no total/pages
- The full paginated endpoint (for a sessions management page) can retain count()
- Consider: either a query param ?dashboard=true to skip count, or a separate dedicated endpoint

### selectinload vs joinedload — use selectinload here:

- selectinload issues a 2nd SELECT ... WHERE id IN (...) — for limit=5 this means 5 UUIDs in one IN clause (not N+1)
- joinedload uses JOIN and can cause Cartesian explosion when combined with LIMIT
- selectinload = 2 queries total regardless of limit value. Correct choice.

### Options order is critical:

- .options() MUST come before .filter() and before .offset()/.limit()
- Count BEFORE applying offset/limit to avoid pagination breakage

### Composite index: consolidate to 0ekz.5 (UPDATED)

- Do NOT add the composite index migration in this bead
- The composite index on test_sessions belongs in the 0ekz.5 migration only (to avoid duplication)
- If a migration is created in this bead, limit it to schema-only changes

## Implementation

In cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py:
  Add patient: PatientInfo | None = None to SessionResponse

In cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py list_sessions:
  1. Add Depends(get_current_doctor) parameter
  2. Add WHERE clinician_user_id = doctor.user_id filter
  3. from sqlalchemy.orm import selectinload
  4. query = db.query(TestSession).options(selectinload(TestSession.patient))
  (options BEFORE filter, count BEFORE offset/limit)
  5. For dashboard-style limit=5 queries: skip count() call

In cognitest/backend/app/models/test_session.py:
  Verify relationship to Patient exists — it should at line 54

## Testing

- [ ] GET /api/v1/sessions/ requires authentication (401 without valid token)
- [ ] Authenticated doctor only sees own sessions (clinician_user_id filter)
- [ ] GET /api/v1/sessions/ returns items with patient.cached_name populated (when patient exists)
- [ ] Sessions with no patient record return patient: null (not 500)
- [ ] Pagination and filtering still work correctly
- [ ] SQL echo confirms exactly 2 queries (not N+1) for a 5-item page

## Validation

- [ ] list_sessions requires get_current_doctor dependency
- [ ] list_sessions filters by clinician_user_id = doctor.user_id
- [ ] SessionResponse.patient.cached_name is a non-null string for sessions with cached patients
- [ ] No N+1 queries (selectinload not lazy load)
- [ ] count() called before offset/limit (pagination correct)

## Files

- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py
- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py
- cognitest/backend/app/models/test_session.py (verify relationship)"}

- selectinload issues a 2nd SELECT ... WHERE id IN (...) — for limit=5 this means 5 UUIDs in one IN clause (not N+1)
- joinedload uses JOIN and can cause Cartesian explosion when combined with LIMIT (SQLAlchemy adds subquery wrapping as compensation — extra planner overhead)

{"description":"## What

Build /dashboard route as the post-login landing page for CogniTest doctors. 2x2 card grid (single column on mobile). Replace /commissions as DEFAULT_POST_LOGIN_ROUTE.

{"description":"## What

Extend POST /api/v1/sessions/ to accept a medsimples_patient_id (integer) in addition to existing patient_id (UUID). When medsimples_patient_id is provided, backend looks up or creates a CogniTest Patient record, then uses that Patient.id for session creation.

## Context

- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py SessionCreate currently requires patient_id: UUID
- cognitest/backend/app/models/patient.py Patient has medsimples_patient_id field and UniqueConstraint(organization_id, medsimples_patient_id)
- Dashboard patient search returns MedSimples integer IDs — frontend has no way to know the CogniTest Patient UUID
- Organization linking: doctor.org_id (str from JWT) is the MedSimples org integer ID

### CRITICAL Security Fixes

**CRITICAL: create_session has no authentication — IDOR on patient_id (sessions.py:120)**
- Current create_session has no get_current_doctor dependency
- Any caller who knows a valid CogniTest patient.id UUID can create sessions for any patient
- Fix: add Depends(get_current_doctor) to create_session AND enforce patient.organization_id == doctor's org after patient lookup

**CRITICAL: get_or_create_default_org is wrong for multi-tenant (sessions.py:33)**
- db.query(Organization).first() returns whichever org comes first — NOT the authenticated doctor's org
- Fix: derive organization_id from get_current_doctor() — look up CogniTest Organization by medsimples_org_id == int(doctor.org_id)
- Only use the default org fallback in single-org dev mode, guarded by settings.dev_mode flag

**CRITICAL: org_id type-safety**
- doctor.org_id is Optional[str] from the JWT claim (e.g. '42')
- Organization.medsimples_org_id is Integer
- Cross-org check MUST use int(doctor.org_id) for comparison — '42' == 42 is False in Python and the guard silently fails
- Add explicit 403 guard if doctor.org_id is None before attempting org lookup

**TOCTOU in get-or-create Patient — must use IntegrityError retry:**
- The UNIQUE(org_id, medsimples_patient_id) constraint prevents data corruption from concurrent requests
- BUT without catching IntegrityError and retrying with SELECT, the second concurrent request returns 500
- Correct pattern:
    try:
        db.add(Patient(...))
        db.flush()
    except IntegrityError:
        db.rollback()
        patient = db.query(Patient).filter_by(org_id=org_id, medsimples_patient_id=ms_patient_id).one()
- Bound retry to 1-2 attempts maximum; do not retry infinitely

**SSRF / httpx — MedSimples API call outside transaction:**
- MedSimples API call to fetch patient name/dob must happen BEFORE the DB transaction (not inside it)
- Use self._shared_client (not per-request httpx.AsyncClient pattern from get_patient())

### Inline resolution pattern (UPDATED: replaces XOR Pydantic validator)

Instead of a model_validator XOR in the schema, use inline resolution at the top of the route handler:
  1. If medsimples_patient_id is provided: resolve to local patient_id at handler start
  2. Proceed with normal session creation using patient_id
  This is simpler, produces clearer error messages, and avoids model_validator(mode='before') complexity.
  patient_id remains required in the schema; route handler resolves medsimples_patient_id before validation.

### Org resolution utility

Create a shared utility function: get_ct_org_for_doctor(doctor: DoctorInfo, db: Session) -> Organization
  - Queries Organization by medsimples_org_id == int(doctor.org_id)
  - Raises 403 if doctor.org_id is None
  - Raises 422 if no matching Organization found
  - Call this from session creation and any future endpoints needing org context
  - This pattern will repeat; extract it rather than inline per endpoint

### Architecture: service layer placement

- The get-or-create Patient logic MUST go in a service layer or repository method
- Do NOT embed it directly in the router (violates established layered architecture: router -> service -> repository)
- Create or use session_service.py / patient_service.py for this logic

### Async httpx for MedSimplesClient:

- get_patient() currently creates a new httpx.AsyncClient per call (TLS handshake cost per call)
- Pass request.app.state.http_client through; use self._shared_client
- MedSimples API call to fetch name/dob should run BEFORE starting the DB transaction

## Implementation

In cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py SessionCreate:
  Add: medsimples_patient_id: int | None = None
  (No XOR validator — resolution handled inline in route handler)

In cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py create_session:
  1. Add Depends(get_current_doctor) — REQUIRED for IDOR fix
  2. If medsimples_patient_id provided:
     a. Resolve org using get_ct_org_for_doctor(doctor, db) utility
        - Raise 422 if org not found
        - Allow dev_mode fallback only if settings.dev_mode is True
     b. Fetch patient data from MedSimples BEFORE transaction (async, don't block transaction)
        - Use self._shared_client in medsimples_client
     c. get-or-create Patient with IntegrityError retry pattern (max 2 attempts)
        - This logic in service layer, not directly in router
  3. Enforce patient.organization_id == ct_org.id after resolution
  4. Use patient.id as session patient_id

Extract: get_ct_org_for_doctor() utility for reuse across endpoints

## Testing

- [ ] POST /sessions/ with medsimples_patient_id=8 creates session and new Patient record
- [ ] Second call with same medsimples_patient_id reuses existing Patient (no duplicate)
- [ ] Concurrent requests for same medsimples_patient_id: both succeed (no 500)
- [ ] POST /sessions/ with patient_id (UUID) still works unchanged
- [ ] POST /sessions/ with neither patient_id nor medsimples_patient_id returns 422
- [ ] Unauthenticated POST /sessions/ returns 401 (not 422)
- [ ] Doctor from org A cannot create session for patient in org B (IDOR test)
- [ ] doctor.org_id = None returns 403 (not AttributeError)
- [ ] If MedSimples fetch fails, session still created with cached_name=None

## Validation

- [ ] Session created successfully with medsimples_patient_id input
- [ ] Patient.cached_name populated from MedSimples fetch
- [ ] No duplicate Patient records for same (org, medsimples_patient_id) even under concurrent load
- [ ] organization_id derived from authenticated doctor, not db.query(Organization).first()
- [ ] int(doctor.org_id) used in comparison (type-safe)

## Files

- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py
- cognitest/backend/app/models/patient.py (verify medsimples_patient_id field)
- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py (use existing get_patient method)
- cognitest/backend/app/utils/org_utils.py or similar (NEW — get_ct_org_for_doctor utility)

## Dependencies

- Requires medsimples-0ekz.5 (UUID→Integer migration) for Organization.medsimples_org_id lookup to work"}

- Dashboard patient search returns MedSimples integer IDs — frontend has no way to know the CogniTest Patient UUID
- Organization linking: doctor.org_id (str from JWT) is the MedSimples org integer ID

### Critical Security Findings from Review

**CRITICAL: create_session has no authentication — IDOR on patient_id (sessions.py:120)**
- Current create_session has no get_current_doctor dependency
- Any caller who knows a valid CogniTest patient.id UUID can create sessions for any patient
- Fix: add Depends(get_current_doctor) to create_session AND enforce patient.organization_id == doctor.org_id after patient lookup

**CRITICAL: get_or_create_default_org is wrong for multi-tenant (sessions.py:33)**
- db.query(Organization).first() returns whichever org comes first — NOT the authenticated doctor's org
- All sessions created via this fallback go under one arbitrary org regardless of who is authenticated
- Fix: derive organization_id from get_current_doctor() — look up CogniTest Organization by medsimples_org_id == int(doctor.org_id)
- Only use the default org fallback in single-org dev mode, guarded by settings.dev_mode flag

**TOCTOU in get-or-create Patient — must use IntegrityError retry:**
- The UNIQUE(org_id, medsimples_patient_id) constraint prevents data corruption from concurrent requests
- BUT without catching IntegrityError and retrying with SELECT, the second concurrent request returns 500 to the user
- Correct pattern:
  try:
      db.add(Patient(...))
      db.flush()
  except IntegrityError:
      db.rollback()
      patient = db.query(Patient).filter_by(org_id=org_id, medsimples_patient_id=ms_patient_id).one()

- The MedSimples API call to fetch name/dob must happen BEFORE the transaction, not inside it (avoids holding transaction open during HTTP call)

### Research Insights

**Pydantic validator for XOR (patient_id OR medsimples_patient_id):**
Use model_validator(mode='after') in Pydantic v2:
  @model_validator(mode='after')
  def exactly_one_patient_identifier(self) -> 'SessionCreate':
      has_local = self.patient_id is not None
      has_remote = self.medsimples_patient_id is not None
      if has_local == has_remote:
          raise ValueError('Provide exactly one of patient_id or medsimples_patient_id')
      return self

**Async httpx for MedSimplesClient:**
- get_patient() currently creates a new httpx.AsyncClient per call (TLS handshake cost per call)
- Pass request.app.state.http_client through to avoid per-request client creation
- MedSimples API call to fetch name/dob should run BEFORE starting the DB transaction

  4. Use patient.id as session patient_id
